name: apply
signature: (apply [f args] [f x args] [f x y args] [f x y z args] [f a b c d & args])

name: map
signature: (map [f] [f coll] [f c1 c2] [f c1 c2 & colls])

name: mapcat
signature: (mapcat [f] [f xs] [f c1 c2] [f c1 c2 & colls])

name: list*
signature: (list* [args] [a args] [a b args] [a b c args] [a b c d & more])

name: juxt
signature: (juxt [f] [f g] [f g h] [f g h & fs])

name: partial
signature: (partial [f] [f arg1] [f arg1 arg2] [f arg1 arg2 arg3] [f arg1 arg2 arg3 & more])

name: update
signature: (update [m k f] [m k f x] [m k f x y] [m k f x y z] [m k f x y z & more])

name: fnil
signature: (fnil [f x] [f x y] [f x y z])

name: some-fn
signature: (some-fn [p] [p1 p2] [p1 p2 p3] [p1 p2 p3 & ps])

name: min-key
signature: (min-key [k x] [k x y] [k x y & more])

name: max-key
signature: (max-key [k x] [k x y] [k x y & more])

name: with-redefs-fn
doc:
Implementation helper for with-redefs. vars is a seq of Vars and vals the
   parallel seq of temporary root values; sets each var's root to its temp
   value, calls (func), then always restores the original roots — returning
   func's value, or re-throwing whatever it threw. Mutates global roots, so it
   works on any var — including non-^:dynamic ones and ordinary defns — unlike
   binding.

name: with-redefs
doc:
(with-redefs [v1 val1 v2 val2 ...] body...) temporarily redefines the root
   values of the named vars for the dynamic extent of body, restoring the
   originals afterward — even if body throws. Unlike binding, it mutates global
   roots and works on any var, including ordinary defns, so use it to stub plain
   functions in tests. Returns the value of body.
